title: Encelia subaristata
taxonomy: Encelia subaristata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is often classified as a member of the genus Encelia, which includes other desert plants. Encelia subaristata is also known to be closely related to other species within the Encelia genus, such as Encelia californica. Further classification may be specific to its subgenus or species within the family Asteraceae.
uses: Encelia subaristata, also known as a desert five-spot or five-petaled bush sunflower, has been found to have various potential medicinal uses. Its roots and leaves are used in traditional medicine by Native American communities to treat a range of ailments, including fever and sore throats.
